pages.raw_text:
Such was the declaration made
to the council last night by Mr.. Os-
borne, a new political agent for
the Washington Water Power Co.,
who appeared in the chambers last
night in place of Harry Blocker,
and Osborn didn't whisper as Har-
ry used to, either. He is evidently
an experiment.


Crack! Councilman N. s. Pratt's
hard fist came down upon the desk


''We will enjoin the company
from any such action. We will go
into the courts about it," he ex-
claimed.


The climax was precipitated by
Councilman E. V. Lambert, the on
ly one by the way who favored
giving the company anything it
wishes in the matter. Lambert
made the assertion that once be-
fore when there was trouble over
the lighting question the company
had left the city in darkness.


Osborne was asked II, in case a
settlement of the problem could not
be reached In a few weeks, the COM
pany would not shut off lights. o.
borne couldn't promise.


If the city had that electric plant
1t might just as we'll have owned
now Osborn and his concern
wouldn't even amount to a faint
ripple.


Osborne has Harry Blocker tied
to a post for nerve displayed. He
calmly stood there and dictated to
8 councilmen as if he were peda-
cog and they kids. He told them
that the company wanted a 5 year
contract for every light that was
installed and that should a light
be put in years after the contract
was made it should still run out
it's years. He was asking for a
self-perpetuating lighting contract,
